what_it_does: Declares a generated surface specification for view B6, a desktop-only surface representing the RHM-twin, anchored at the address ui://twin, and lists build targets for the component twin, a design-lint command, and a token-to-CSS pipeline.
observations: Status is quality-passed but home_journey is _unrouted and journeys/cross_journeys are empty.
Only one address is listed: ui://twin.
build_to names component twin, a lint command, and a tokens-to-CSS source.
Title references model-of-Tim plus provenance grading.
standouts: Quality-passed surface with no routed journeys or cross-journeys.
Explicit build_to section coupling the surface to lint and token build steps.
conventions: Follows the surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, and build_to.
Uses a ui:// URI as an address reference.
References external build commands and token files.
concerns: Empty journeys and cross_journeys may indicate an unrouted placeholder rather than a fully wired surface.
build_to command paths are relative and not validated within this file.
No symbols or implementation details are recorded.
notes: B6 is a quality-passed but unrouted desktop surface spec for the RHM-twin, pinned to ui://twin with build instructions for component, lint, and tokens.
questions: Why is status quality-passed while home_journey is _unrouted and journeys/cross_journeys are empty?
What do RHM-twin, model-of-Tim, and provenance grading denote in this context?
What is the intended behavior when the token or lint build steps fail?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a surface spec whose journeys/cross_journeys carry the web of addresses; actual content records the surface and build targets but carries no journey references, so it is a routing/build anchor rather than a wired node.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a buildable desktop UI surface for a digital twin of Tim with provenance grading, anchored to a single address and wired to lint and token build steps.
novelty: The build_to block explicitly links the surface to a lint command and a token-to-CSS source, and the title combines model-of-Tim with provenance grading.
contribution: A surface-level contract that names the RHM-twin view, its platform scope, its address, and its build targets, which a fused system would keep as a routing and build anchor regardless of how journey refs are later populated.
summary_for_embedding: B6.json is a generated desktop surface specification for the RHM-twin view, anchored at ui://twin, with empty journey arrays and build targets for component twin, design lint, and design-system tokens.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to record a quality-passed, build-routable surface for the twin/provenance-grading view and its associated build pipeline.
